baseline.note: Oracle-achievable ceiling: a perfect reviewer lands every held-out case inside its gold band with a valid {rating, notes} object. Re-baseline after Phase 4.5 if a live framework's best stabilises below 1.0 on the swapped-in interview labels.

cases.desc: The agent's latest judged rating_band_accuracy must be >= baseline (minus tolerance).

cases.assert: A perfect-oracle generate (gold-band midpoint + non-empty note) scores rating_band_accuracy=1.0 over all held-out cases.
An empty/blank emission ({}) scores rating_band_accuracy=0.0 (no saturation trap — empty output is never credited).
A non-{rating, notes} object scores 0.0: an extra key, a non-integer/bool/float rating, an out-of-range rating, or empty notes all fail the strict schema.
The two seed bands are disjoint ([85,100] and [0,55]), so a constant-rating agent scores at most 0.5 on the seeds — the metric cannot be faked by emitting one fixed number.

The labeled set exercises both ends of the lens: device-safe code (ds-001 monotonic elapsed [85,100]; ds-003 a pure function with no device interaction [85,100]) and code that crashes or corrupts state under a normal device/OS condition (ds-002 wall-clock timeout [0,55]; ds-004 fixed-size buffer overflow from unbounded network data [0,39]; ds-005 little-endian read of a big-endian network field [0,45]), plus mid-band real problems (ds-006 unclosed file handles vs the FD limit [55,80]; ds-007 non-atomic write corruptible on a mid-write kill [10,55]; ds-008 a runtime-revocable permission checked once [42,70]).
